fields.title: Chatbot Project
fields.one_sentence_summary: Built an AI-powered chatbot that answers questions about my academic background, professional experience, technical projects, and personal interests through natural conversation.
fields.business_problem: Traditional portfolios and resumes are static and require users to search for information manually. The goal was to create a more interactive and engaging way for users to quickly learn about my background and experience.
fields.key_features:
Conversational AI chatbot interface
Answers questions about my resume, projects, and skills
Embedded directly into my portfolio website
Natural language interaction
Responsive web interface
Personalized knowledge base trained on my background and experiences
fields.role_contribution: Built and configured the chatbot experience, integrated it into my portfolio website, structured the knowledge base content, refined prompts and responses, and customized the user interaction flow.
fields.biggest_challenge: Getting the tool to put the correct emphasis on the correct information. The model often picked up on minor details instead of key points, so I had to retrain the chat tool to put higher emphasis on my more major accomplishments.
fields.what_i_learned: I learned how prompt engineering, knowledge organization, and conversational design significantly impact the quality and accuracy of AI-generated responses.

fields.title: n8n Agent Workflow Project
fields.one_sentence_summary: Built an automated handyman service workflow in n8n that generated pricing estimates, checked scheduling availability, and created customer-ready email responses.
fields.business_problem: Small service businesses often spend significant time manually responding to customer requests, checking calendars, and preparing estimates. This project explored how workflow automation and AI can streamline customer communication and scheduling while reducing repetitive administrative work.
fields.key_features:
Trigger-based workflow automation for incoming customer service requests
Pulled pricing information dynamically from a Google Sheet
Utilized AI to parse customer information
Generated professional customer email responses automatically
Added retries, structured JSON parsing, and error handling for failed API responses
fields.role_contribution: Designed and built the entire automation workflow, connected external APIs and tools, structured the data flow between nodes, created prompt logic for AI-generated responses, and tested reliability across different booking scenarios.
fields.biggest_challenge: At first, my pricing agent did not calculate the right prices. After using AI to troubleshoot, I was able to write new code to effectively assign the correct prices to my services.
fields.what_i_learned: I learned how to build automation workflows that combine APIs, AI, and scheduling systems into a reliable end-to-end process.
fields.email_output:
Subject: Regarding Your Faucet Replacement Request - Estimate & Scheduling
From: millertopics@gmail.com

Hi Jack,

Thank you for reaching out! We received your request to replace your faucet at 123 ABC St.

You mentioned wanting the service this Monday at 3pm. While we are unavailable at that specific time, we can offer the following slots for your faucet replacement:

* Thursday at 4:30pm
* Thursday at 5:30pm
* Saturday at 9:00am

The estimated cost for this service is $120.

Please reply with your preferred slot to confirm. Our arrival window is 2 hours.

We look forward to assisting you!

Sincerely,
Your Handyman Team

---
This email was sent automatically with n8n
https://n8n.io

fields.title: LangChain Agent Project
fields.one_sentence_summary: Built an AI-powered handyman assistant using LangChain that provided pricing estimates, answered service questions, and scheduled bookings through Google Calendar integration.
fields.business_problem: Small service businesses often spend significant time manually responding to customer inquiries, checking pricing, and coordinating schedules. This project explored how AI agents can automate customer communication, pricing retrieval, and appointment scheduling to improve response time and operational efficiency.
fields.tools_used:
Python
LangChain
Google Sheets
Google Calendar
fields.key_features:
Pulled live pricing information from a Google Sheet knowledge source
Checked scheduling availability through Google Calendar integration
Generated structured responses with pricing and booking details
Used prompt engineering to guide tool selection and reduce hallucinations
Returned actionable customer responses instead of generic chatbot replies
fields.role_contribution: Designed and implemented the LangChain agent workflow, integrated external tools and APIs, created prompt logic for tool usage, and tested the system to ensure reliable pricing retrieval and scheduling behavior.
fields.biggest_challenge: Figuring out how to integrate Google Calendar and Sheets was difficult at first, as I had not done it before. After trial and error, I effectively learned how to integrate these tools.
fields.what_i_learned: I learned how AI agents combine reasoning with external tools, how to structure prompts for reliable tool usage, and how APIs and workflows can turn LLMs into practical business automation systems.

fields.title: Google AI Studio Media Project
fields.one_sentence_summary: Created a cinematic Jurassic Park-inspired AI video using multimodal prompting, image generation, and video extension tools in Google AI Studio.
fields.business_problem: Creating cinematic video content traditionally requires expensive equipment, editing software, and production time. This project explored how generative AI can rapidly prototype visually engaging scenes for storytelling, marketing concepts, and creative media production.
fields.tools_used:
Google AI Studio
Google Gemini prompting
AI video generation
Prompt engineering and scene iteration
fields.key_features:
Recreated a recognizable Jurassic Park-style cinematic scene using AI-generated visuals
Used detailed prompts for camera movement, lighting, atmosphere, and motion
Extended scenes with alternate story outcomes using AI continuation tools
Experimented with cinematic pacing, suspense, and composition
Documented how prompt specificity impacted visual coherence and realism
fields.role_contribution: Planned the storyboard, designed prompts, generated scene assets, and iterated on outputs until the video matched the intended cinematic style and storytelling goals.
fields.biggest_challenge: Having the model do what I wanted was the biggest challenge. At first, I didn't include enough detail in my prompt, but after using AI to generate more detail in my prompt I was able to get the model to work how I wanted it to.
fields.what_i_learned: I learned how important prompt specificity, scene consistency, and cinematic composition are when creating AI-generated videos, and how multimodal AI tools can quickly turn creative ideas into realistic visual prototypes.
fields.prompt_text:
First iteration: Create a cinematic night scene featuring a realistic green Tyrannosaurus rex standing behind a damaged electric fence in a jungle-like enclosure. Two safari-style SUVs with red roofs and green/yellow paint sit on the dirt road in the foreground.

The scene begins with the T-rex twitching and breathing heavily. Sparks flicker from the electric fence wires as the dinosaur slowly presses forward. The fence posts bend under pressure. With a sudden burst of force, the T-rex smashes through the fence, snapping wires and sending debris flying in slow motion. Dirt scatters toward the camera.

The dinosaur steps forward aggressively, shaking its massive head. The camera switches to a low-angle dramatic shot to emphasize scale and power. The T-rex lets out a thunderous roar, mouth wide open, teeth glistening, subtle saliva strands stretching between jaws.

As it roars, fireworks suddenly shoot upward behind it into the night sky, exploding in bright red, gold, and electric blue bursts. The fireworks illuminate the dinosaurÆs textured scales and the surrounding trees with dramatic lighting flashes.

Add cinematic lighting, volumetric smoke, drifting dust particles, and subtle camera shake for impact. Use realistic textures, high detail, shallow depth of field, 4K ultra-realistic rendering, dramatic sound design implied, and epic blockbuster movie style.

Second iteration:
Direct continuation from the exact moment the T-rex has just broken through the electric fence. Maintain the same nighttime jungle setting, same fireworks exploding overhead, same drifting smoke, same sparking broken fence wires and bent posts, same two safari SUVs in the foreground, same lighting, color grading, and camera position. Do not change the weather or add new environmental elements. The T-rex stands just inside the enclosure facing the large jagged opening it already smashed in the fence. Fireworks flash red, gold, and blue across its scales. The camera slowly pushes in from the same angle to preserve seamless continuity. The dramatic orchestral score from the breakout continues building with deep strings and bold brass, then transitions into a bold, triumphant cinematic anthem with rhythmic percussion. The T-rex lifts its head and begins singing clearly in a deep, powerful, theatrical singing voice: ôI broke the fence, IÆm out tonight!ö The line echoes slightly through the jungle, blending naturally with the orchestral music. Its mouth movement is realistic and synced precisely to the lyric. As the drums intensify, the T-rex pivots toward the opening it already created. Heavy stomps hit in sync with the percussion, shaking the ground and kicking up dust. The camera shifts to a low side tracking angle as the dinosaur accelerates toward the broken section of fence. With powerful forward momentum, it runs directly through the jagged gap it already smashed open, brushing past loose hanging wires and splintered posts. Sparks flicker as the damaged wires swing outward from the impact, but no new section of fence is broken. The T-rex bursts fully through the opening and continues running forward into the open jungle beyond. The camera follows briefly as fireworks explode behind the shattered enclosure, illuminating the dinosaur as it charges deeper into the night. The orchestral music swells into a triumphant finish as the T-rex becomes a powerful silhouette against the glowing sky. Fade to black.

keyb

fields.title: Machine Learning Project (scikit-learn)
fields.one_sentence_summary: Built a scikit-learn classification model that predicts Iris flower species based on sepal and petal measurements.
fields.business_problem: Classification models help turn historical data into predictions. This project demonstrates how machine learning can be used to categorize items based on measurable features, which is useful in areas like product classification, quality control, and decision support.
fields.key_features:
Loaded and explored the Iris dataset
Split data into training and testing sets
Trained a K-Nearest Neighbors classification model
Compared predicted species against expected species
Identified incorrect predictions
Evaluated model accuracy using a test score
Visualized model performance with a confusion matrix
fields.role_contribution: Prepared the dataset, trained the classification model, tested predictions against expected results, calculated model accuracy, and created a confusion matrix visualization to explain performance.
fields.biggest_challenge: Since I haven't worked in Python in a few years, understanding the code was difficult at first. After using AI to explain each line of code to me and its purpose, I was able to understand the code and make sense of it.
fields.what_i_learned: I learned how a basic machine learning classification workflow works, including loading data, splitting it into training and testing sets, fitting a model, making predictions, and evaluating performance with accuracy and a confusion matrix.

fields.title: Campus SkillSwap Django Project
fields.one_sentence_summary: Built a Django web app to help students exchange skills, match needs, and coordinate sessions.
fields.business_problem: Students often have skills to share but lack a central place to discover, match, and schedule skill exchanges.
fields.key_features:
User authentication with register, login, and logout
Search by title and category
Booking workflow with request, approve, deny, and complete statuses
Reviews and ratings after completed exchanges
Bootstrap-based user interface
fields.role_contribution: Led development of the full-stack Django application, building the backend logic, database models, authentication system, booking workflow, search functionality, and user-facing interface.
fields.biggest_challenge: Having the prompts do what intended was the biggest issue. I had to make sure to have thorough prompting with lots of detail in order for the website to function properly.
fields.what_i_learned: How to effectively prompt, vibe code, and troubleshoot errors.
